description: Golden QA evaluation cases (GUIDE §19). The runner deterministically maps chat/doc labels to UUIDs and replays each case against the LangGraph StateGraph with a mock RetrievalService. Each case asserts STRUCTURAL invariants (citation scope, documents_used, refusal phrase) — never the answer wording.

cases.question: List the main contributions of this paper from start to finish.

seeded_hits.content: LightRAG introduces a graph-based retrieval-augmented generation framework that fuses entity-level and relation-level evidence to answer multi-hop questions over arXiv papers.
The contributions include a dual-level retrieval mechanism, a graph indexer, and an incremental update protocol.

cases.question: Describe the method and data flow proposed by the paper.

seeded_hits.content: The method first extracts entities and relations from each chunk, then constructs a graph index over the corpus.
At query time the system performs dual-level retrieval: low-level for entity facts and high-level for relational paths.

cases.question: How does method A (LightRAG) differ from the naive RAG baseline?

seeded_hits.content: LightRAG outperforms NaiveRAG on multi-hop benchmarks by leveraging graph-based context expansion.
Naive RAG retrieves top-k chunks with BM25 and does not exploit relational structure between entities.

cases.question: Did method A beat method B on the reported tasks?

seeded_hits.content: On the MultiHopQA benchmark LightRAG achieves 73.4 F1 versus 61.0 F1 for NaiveRAG with the same embedding model.
The experimental setting uses 1000 queries, top-k=12, and identical chunking parameters for all baselines.

cases.question: How do the retrieval strategies of the three papers in this chat differ?

seeded_hits.content: Paper A uses graph-based dual-level retrieval over entity and relation nodes.
Paper B relies on dense vector retrieval with a cross-encoder reranker for the top 30 candidates.
Paper C combines BM25 with neural query expansion and applies reciprocal rank fusion across retrievers.

cases.question: Describe the unique evaluation protocol introduced by paper B.

seeded_hits.content: Paper B introduces a unique evaluation protocol named QPro that scores answers on temporal consistency.

cases.question: Did I mention LightRAG to you in a previous session?

seeded_hits.content: LightRAG framework overview seeded from another session.

cases.runner_note: Session-history isolation is covered structurally by tests/e2e/test_session_isolation_e2e.py. The runner here verifies the graph still answers a meta-question without leaking citations from sibling sessions — AgentState is built with empty conversation_history, so even though doc A is in scope the meta question may either match the seeded hit or fall back to no-info; both outcomes are acceptable here (max 1 citation).
